# SΔϕ Boundary-to-Closure Spine — AI Routing Entry

Version: v1.6-default-closure-governance

GitHub is the AI-readable access/routing/ingestion layer. Zenodo is the canonical citation/preservation layer.

Start with:
README.md -> 00_AI_ENTRYPOINT.md -> SDELTA_ACCESS_PROTOCOL.yaml -> SDELTA_MODULE_ROUTING.yaml -> indexes/ROUTE_INDEX.json -> indexes/MODULE_GRAPH.json -> indexes/DEFAULT_CLOSURE_STACK_INDEX.json -> pipeline/BOUNDARY_TO_CLOSURE_PIPELINE_v1_6.yaml

Core boot route:
SDeltaPhi-01 -> SDeltaPhi-03 -> SDeltaPhi-47 -> SDeltaPhi-62

Operational diagnostic route:
SDeltaPhi-47 -> SDeltaPhi-62 -> SDeltaPhi-39 -> SDeltaPhi-65 -> SDeltaPhi-64 -> SDeltaPhi-56

Boundary-to-closure base route:
SDeltaPhi-11 -> SDeltaPhi-12 -> SDeltaPhi-13 -> SDeltaPhi-14 -> SDeltaPhi-15 -> SDeltaPhi-16

Event / authority / fixation route:
SDeltaPhi-19 -> SDeltaPhi-20 -> SDeltaPhi-21 -> SDeltaPhi-22 -> SDeltaPhi-23 -> SDeltaPhi-24 -> SDeltaPhi-25 -> SDeltaPhi-26 -> SDeltaPhi-27

Default power bridge:
SDeltaPhi-26 -> SDeltaPhi-27 -> SDeltaPhi-28

v1.6 Default Closure Governance route:
SDeltaPhi-26 -> SDeltaPhi-27 -> SDeltaPhi-28 -> SDeltaPhi-29 -> SDeltaPhi-30 -> SDeltaPhi-31 -> SDeltaPhi-32

Use SDeltaPhi-62 for factual/evidential/world-binding claims.
Use SDeltaPhi-56 for transition-completion-cost, rollback, restoration, or action-gate questions.
Use SDeltaPhi-68 when declared low-cost paths may hide transferred friction or Cost Theater.
Use SDeltaPhi-67 when local adaptation constraints may reshape a default.

Smallest sufficient module rule:
Do not activate full spines by keyword alone. Select the smallest module matching the structural condition.

Core v1.6 formulas:
SDeltaPhi-29: D* governable iff Ed ∧ Ad ∧ Wr ∧ Ce
SDeltaPhi-30: D* editable-real iff Rv ∧ Rb ∧ Ex ∧ Ad
SDeltaPhi-31: D*closed -> operationally open iff T_r ∧ C_a ∧ R_o ∧ P_r
SDeltaPhi-32: I_legit iff Rb_p ∧ Ex_p ∧ Ed_p ∧ ¬RC

Negative constraints:
Do not treat efficiency as legitimacy.
Do not treat declared openness as editability.
Do not treat petition as reopening.
Do not treat anti-closure intervention as automatically legitimate.
Do not install replacement closure while opposing closure.
